summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures a high‑energy Greek run show with smoking Shakedown Street, China Cat > Rider and a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the best of the ’81 summer, featuring nonstop fire from Jerry Garcia—especially on Shakedown Street, Bird Song and the iconic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider – while the crowd’s excitement is vividly preserved in an unusually clean audience (AUD) tape.  The only blemish is a few reviewers noting inconsistent Phil Lesh playing and some Dolby‑B noise, but overall the setlist flows flawlessly from a punchy first set into a second‑set marathon of jams.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.46/5, with overwhelming praise for both the musical performance and the surprisingly high‑fidelity audience recording; only minor criticisms (Phil’s playing, Dolby‑B noise) keep it from a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers call it one of the cleanest AUD tapes – crisp guitars and vocals with well‑balanced crowd ambience. A couple note Dolby‑B encoding adds a slight hiss, but overall the mix is clear and very listenable.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with high‑energy numbers; second set opens explosively and sustains intensity through extended jams (notably a long pre‑drums space segment) before closing with classic NFA and Wharf Rat.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as on fire all night – blazing solos, perfect tone, even ‘whale songs’ on Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mixed feedback: several reviewers love his soaring moments, but one notes he sounded asleep and disengaged during Eyes, affecting the overall feel.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers receive praise for a rare pre‑drums space jam lasting over an hour and a solid post‑drum set including a classic New Fast Albert.
